The Engineering of the Lake Pontchartrain Causeway - YouTube
hits.summary:
The Lake Pontchartrain Causeway stretches nearly 24 miles across open water, connecting New Orleans to Louisiana’s North Shore.
When it opened in 1956, it was the longest continuous bridge over water in the world — and it still holds that record today.
...

{ts:53} longest continuous bridge over water in the world.

{ts:386} completed span measured 23.86 miles in length.
Each deck section was 56 feet in length.
In total, it required more than
{ts:395} 2200 precast spans and roughly 4500 foundation piles driven deep into the lake bed to hold it in place.
...
The second span stretched just slightly longer at 23.87
{ts:508} miles and replaced the original 56-ft deck sections with longer 84-ft spans, this time supported by three pile bands
{ts:517} rather than two, reducing the total precast segments to 1,500 from the 2,200 used before.

Given that the contract required construction of the 24-mile span in only 23 months, the engineers devised a rather simple structure - two piles straddled by a bent cap supporting the ends of two 56 foot decks.
...
The record shows that the bridge was constructed in fourteen months from the time the first piles were driven on May 23, 1955.
The original (now Southbound) span opened to traffic on August 30, 1956.
